$comment: Neutral shipped <time.h> (ISO C + POSIX _r) — c16 (SQLite os_unix date/time surface). SQLite uses time/localtime/localtime_r/gmtime/gmtime_r/strftime/clock + struct tm (it reads tm_sec..tm_year) + time_t/clock_t. ★ struct tm is SIZE-CRITICAL: SQLite stack-allocates a `struct tm` and passes &tm to localtime_r, so libc writes the FULL 56 bytes — a short struct would overflow the caller's frame. Verified against glibc time/bits/types/struct_tm.h + Darwin (apple-oss-distributions Libc include/_time.h — the <time.h> include chain; RE-AUDITED c83 against that exact file: 9 int fields tm_sec..tm_isdst, `long tm_gmtoff`, `char *tm_zone` — the same 11 fields in the same order): BYTE-IDENTICAL on glibc and Darwin (LP64) — `int tm_sec..tm_isdst` (9 ints, offsets 0..32), 4 bytes pad (the layout engine derives it to 8-align the next field), `long tm_gmtoff` @40, `char* tm_zone` @48 → sizeof 56, 8-aligned. So `struct tm` is FLAT (no per-target variant). `struct timespec` is ALSO byte-identical (c83 re-audit: xnu bsd/sys/_types/_timespec.h {__darwin_time_t tv_sec; long tv_nsec} = {i64,i64} = glibc's {__time_t, __syscall_slong_t} on LP64 → flat {i64,i64}, sizeof 16 — UNLIKE sys/time.json's timeval, whose Darwin tv_usec is __int32_t and needs per-format variants [D-FFI-MACHO-TIMEVAL-TV-USEC-WIDTH]). time_t = i64 on both (LP64 signed long). clock_t diverges in SIGNEDNESS only (glibc `long`=i64 / Darwin `unsigned long`=u64) — same 8-byte width, shipped as a typedef `variants` (honest; SQLite's `clock_t x = clock()` works either way). The time_t*/struct tm* params + the struct-tm* returns are typed ptr<void> (standard C pointer coercion) so the layout isn't baked into a signature; the clock() return is i64 (ABI-identical to u64 — same register width). Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho] (the FULL-UNIX-build targets; a windows-pe build uses os_win, a separate axis — MSVCRT time_t/struct tm differ and localtime_r→localtime_s, deferred to that axis). c53 += nanosleep(const struct timespec*, struct timespec*) — both params ptr<void> per house style; the timespec struct (below, shipped c46) is its req/rem. â c82 (shell.c): C89 7.23.1 REQUIRES time.h to provide size_t + NULL without stddef.h; width/value identical to stddef.json, first-wins dedup makes the multi-header provision moot.
